card.text: Reorder by dropping onto another card

model.signal:
...
$source.remove(); $target.parent.insertBefore($source, $target);

x-chapter-heading.title: Behaviors
x-chapter-heading.subtitle: use:* attaches small reusable extensions; drag:* / drop:* compose into drag-and-drop.

div.p: A behavior is a function attached to a single element. It returns a Disposable so it cleans up on the next render. Built-ins:

ul.li: drag:source="$x" + drop:target="$y" + drop:command="cmd" - run a command with $source/$target in scope on drop.

div.p: Add your own with language.behaviors.set('name', fn) in a feature plugin. Each function receives (element, value, instance, local) and returns a Disposable.

button.title: Open this snippet in a new tab

div.small: Click Copy; the button briefly turns green and the value lands in your clipboard.

div.p: A behavior is just a function with the signature (element, value, instance, local) => Disposable. Register it once with language.behaviors.set('name', fn); from then on, any view can use it via use:name="...". The runtime collects the returned Disposable on the active render scope, so it is detached on the next render or unmount automatically.
div.small: All three buttons share the same behavior; each gets its own listener pair and its own Disposable cleanup.
Drag a card onto another to reorder. The drag is wired by attaching three attributes and writing a single command - no event handlers in JS.
